adds.rationale: Watchlist is saturated with AI mega-caps (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA) plus broad-market SPY, with zero exposure to the profile's explicit next waves (robotics, rockets/spacecraft, nuclear). ARKQ (ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF) is the most direct, liquid way to advance the 'invest early in next waves while cheap' rule. Holdings span industrial robotics, autonomous vehicles, 3D printing, and space/aerospace names — covering two of the profile's next-wave buckets in one ticker. ADV well above the $5M liquidity floor.

news_evidence.summary: ARK Invest's autonomous tech and robotics ETF ARKQ has gathered roughly $3B in AUM amid a surge of investor interest in disruptive-innovation strategies through Q1 2021, with strong inflows into the robotics theme.

Global semiconductor shortage is accelerating capital spending on factory automation and industrial robotics across autos and electronics manufacturers, with Reuters noting record order books at robotics suppliers.

removes.rationale: SPY's largest weights at this date are AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, FB, GOOGL, NVDA — the watchlist already holds the top mega-caps directly, so SPY is the most redundant slot. Removing it frees a slot for thematic next-wave exposure that the rest of the watchlist cannot provide. The optimizer can still allocate AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA directly.

news_evidence.summary: S&P 500 closed Q1 2021 up 5.8% on the quarter, driven heavily by the same mega-cap tech names already in the watchlist; concentration in top-10 names remains near multi-decade highs per WSJ Markets coverage.

rationale_overall: Watchlist is at the cap of 5 and already overweight current-wave AI mega-caps via direct AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA positions. The single highest-value move is swapping SPY (redundant beta given the mega-cap holdings already cover SPY's top weights) for ARKQ, which adds robotics and adjacent-autonomy/space exposure — directly advancing the profile's rule (2) 'invest early in next waves while cheap.' Conservative single swap rather than churning multiple slots; AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA remain to keep current-wave AI exposure intact per rule (1).
